content: Nishanth R is an AI Engineer specializing in Agentic AI systems, multi-agent orchestration, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). He is currently an AI Research Fellow at IIIT Hyderabad (CCNSB) under Prof. Deva Priyakumar, focusing on Graph Neural Networks for drug discovery.
Agentic AI (ReAct, Reflexion, MCP), Multi-agent orchestration, RAG, Hybrid Search (BM25 + Vector Search + RRF), FastAPI, Distributed Systems, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Gemini/OpenAI API.

ChainMind is a production-grade multi-agent, self-healing supply chain intelligence platform. It utilizes a distributed architecture with an Orchestrator Agent, specialist domains (Demand, Inventory, Procurement, Logistics, QA), and a ReAct loop (Think-Act-Observe-Verify). It implements a hybrid RAG system with BM25 + Dense (ChromaDB) fusion and a fault-tolerant LLM router with failover chains and circuit breakers.

Synergex Med is an AI-powered quality analysis system for healthcare phone calls. It replaced manual QA processes for 39+ clinics by analyzing transcripts and producing structured assessments using Gemini 2.5 Flash and FastAPI. It ensures HIPAA compliance and agent performance monitoring.

A production-grade healthcare RAG assistant with built-in PII/PHI compliance. It features an NER-based masking engine using Microsoft Presidio to redact patient names, SSNs, and MRNs. It uses LangChain, FastAPI, and pgvector for high-precision clinical Q&A with a complete clinical audit log.

DocKnowledge allows users to upload PDF and text files for natural language querying. It features a scalable backend (FastAPI, pgvector) for semantic search over embeddings and grounded generation with source citations (file/page numbers).

An autonomous multi-agent news curator that aggregates trending AI papers and models. It uses OpenAI GPT-4 with Chain-of-Thought reasoning to score article relevance and convert unstructured HTML/transcripts into clean Markdown.
A Dynamo-inspired distributed key-value store built in Python. It demonstrates core CS fundamentals like Consistent Hashing, Quorum-based replication, and Write-Ahead Logging (WAL).

At IIIT-H (CCNSB), Nishanth redesigned GNN architectures improving MAE by 6-8%. He worked on Graph Transformers (MAF: 0.37, RMSE: 0.65) and molecular graph modeling for drug discovery under Prof. Deva Priyakumar.
Nishanth has 89 repositories including contributions to scikit-learn, PyTorch, and LangChain. He is active in the open-source community as a contributor to major AI frameworks.

Email: nishanth0962333@gmail.com. Phone: +91 6302092732. Location: Hyderabad, India. Portfolio: nishanth-nishu.github.io. LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/r-nishanth-/. GitHub: github.com/Nishanth-nishu. LeetCode: leetcode.com/u/nishanth_2317.
